(天皇賞; lit. 'Emperor Prize') refers to two [horse races](/wiki/Horse_race "Horse race") held yearly in [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"), once in the [spring](/wiki/Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D_(Spring) "Tennō Shō (Spring)") and once in the [autumn](/wiki/Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D_(Autumn) "Tennō Shō (Autumn)"). *Tennō* refers to the [emperor of Japan](/wiki/Emperor_of_Japan "Emperor of Japan"). The races are both International [Grade I races](/wiki/Group_races "Group races"). Prior to the 2007 races, both Tennō Shō races were Japanese domestic Grade 1 races.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

["2011.10.25 Tenno Sho (Autumn) (G1) - Preview - News - Horse Racing in Japan"](http://japanracing.jp/_news2011/111025-02.html). Japanracing.jp. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20120713015452/http://japanracing.jp/_news2011/111025-02.html) from the original on 2012-07-13. Retrieved 2012-03-25.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Tenno Sho](/w/index.php?title=Tenno_Sho&redirect=no "Tenno Sho"))

Horse races held in Japan

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Translation_to_english_arrow.svg) | You can help **expand this article with text translated from [the corresponding article](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E5%A4%A9%E7%9A%87%E8%B3%9E "ja:天皇賞") in Japanese**. *(January 2026)* Click [show] for important translation instructions.  * Consider **[adding a topic](/wiki/Template:Expand_Japanese#Topics_and_categorization "Template:Expand Japanese")** to this template: there are already 1,398 articles in the [main category](/wiki/Category:Articles_needing_translation_from_Japanese_Wikipedia "Category:Articles needing translation from Japanese Wikipedia"), and specifying`|topic=` will aid in categorization. * Do not translate text that appears unreliable or low-quality. If possible, verify the text with references provided in the foreign-language article. * You **must** provide [copyright attribution](/wiki/Wikipedia:Copying_within_Wikipedia "Wikipedia:Copying within Wikipedia") in the [edit summary](/wiki/Help:Edit_summary "Help:Edit summary") accompanying your translation by providing an [interlanguage link](/wiki/Help:Interlanguage_links "Help:Interlanguage links") to the source of your translation. A model attribution edit summary is `Content in this edit is translated from the existing Japanese Wikipedia article at [[:Ja:天皇賞]]; see its history for attribution.` * You may also add the template `{{Translated|Ja|天皇賞}}` to the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D "Talk:Tennō Shō"). * For more guidance, see [Wikipedia:Translation](/wiki/Wikipedia:Translation "Wikipedia:Translation"). |

Main articles: [Tennō Shō (Spring)](/wiki/Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D_(Spring) "Tennō Shō (Spring)") and [Tennō Shō (Autumn)](/wiki/Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D_(Autumn) "Tennō Shō (Autumn)")

The **Tennō Shō** (天皇賞; lit. 'Emperor Prize') refers to two [horse races](/wiki/Horse_race "Horse race") held yearly in [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"), once in the [spring](/wiki/Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D_(Spring) "Tennō Shō (Spring)") and once in the [autumn](/wiki/Tenn%C5%8D_Sh%C5%8D_(Autumn) "Tennō Shō (Autumn)"). *Tennō* refers to the [emperor of Japan](/wiki/Emperor_of_Japan "Emperor of Japan"). The races are both International [Grade I races](/wiki/Group_races "Group races"). Prior to the 2007 races, both Tennō Shō races were Japanese domestic Grade 1 races.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Prior to 1980, a horse winning a Tennō Shō race was not allowed to participate in future editions of the race but this ban was lifted in 1981. Only two horses won the prize three times:

* [Kitasan Black](/wiki/Kitasan_Black "Kitasan Black") (2016 Spring, 2017 Spring and Autumn)
* [T. M. Opera O](/wiki/T._M._Opera_O "T. M. Opera O") (2000 Spring and Autumn, 2001 Spring)

Apart from Kitasan Black and T. M. Opera O, four horses to date have won consecutive runnings, either by winning both the Spring and Autumn races in the same year or by winning the Autumn race, and following year's Spring race.

* [Tamamo Cross](/wiki/Tamamo_Cross "Tamamo Cross") (1988 Spring, Autumn)
* [Super Creek](/wiki/Super_Creek "Super Creek") (1989 Autumn, 1990 Spring)
* [Special Week](/wiki/Special_Week "Special Week") (1999 Spring, Autumn)
* [Meisho Samson](/wiki/Meisho_Samson "Meisho Samson") (2007 Spring and Autumn)

Five horses have won two non-consecutive runnings.

* [Mejiro McQueen](/wiki/Mejiro_McQueen "Mejiro McQueen") (1991, 1992 Spring, finished 1st in 1991 Autumn but demoted to last)
* [Rice Shower](/wiki/Rice_Shower "Rice Shower") (1993, 1995 Spring)
* [Symboli Kris S](/wiki/Symboli_Kris_S "Symboli Kris S") (2002, 2003 Autumn)
* [Fenomeno](/wiki/Fenomeno_(horse) "Fenomeno (horse)") (2013, 2014 Spring)
* [Fierement](/wiki/Fierement "Fierement") (2019, 2020 Spring)
* [Almond Eye](/wiki/Almond_Eye "Almond Eye") (2019, 2020 Autumn)
* [Equinox](/wiki/Equinox_(horse) "Equinox (horse)") (2022, 2023 Autumn)
